title: Positive-rate PCA and IPS with stationary Bernoulli measures are rapidly forgetful
abstract: We prove that every probabilistic cellular automaton with strictly positive transition probabilities that admits a stationary Bernoulli measure is exponentially ergodic. Moreover, the mixing time of any finite region in such a system is logarithmic in the diameter of the region. A similar result holds in continuous time for positive-rate, finite-range interacting particle systems. The proofs use entropy, and rely on a representation of the system as a perturbation of another system with noise. The ergodic behaviour results from a competition between the accumulation of randomness due to noise and the diffusion of randomness due to local information exchange. We show that, in two and higher dimensions, the positive-rate probabilistic cellular automata that admit stationary Bernoulli measures are algorithmically indistinguishable from those that do not.
summary: The paper proves that stochastic lattice models like PCA and IPS converge exponentially fast to their stationary Bernoulli measures if the transition rules are strictly positive. It also demonstrates that in two or more dimensions, it is computationally impossible to distinguish between systems that have such a stationary measure and those that do not.
main_problem: Investigating the ergodicity, convergence properties, and algorithmic complexity of Probabilistic Cellular Automata (PCA) and Interacting Particle Systems (IPS) that admit stationary Bernoulli measures.
main_result: The authors prove that positive-rate PCA and IPS admitting stationary Bernoulli measures are exponentially ergodic with logarithmic mixing times, and show that in dimensions d >= 2, these systems are algorithmically indistinguishable from those that do not admit such measures.
method: An information-theoretic approach using relative entropy (Kullback-Leibler divergence) as a Lyapunov function, combined with noise decomposition and strong data processing inequalities.
model_or_system: Discrete-time Probabilistic Cellular Automable (PCA) with synchronous updates and continuous-time Interacting Particle Systems (IPS) with asynchronous updates on a d-dimensional lattice.
key_observables: Relative entropy (Kullback-Leibler divergence), total variation distance, and mixing time.
important_parameters: Positive transition rates (noise strength), lattice dimension d, and the diameter of the observed region n.
main_assumptions: The systems must have strictly positive transition probabilities (positive-rate) and admit a stationary Bernoulli measure.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of ergodicity in PCA/IPS, develops a noise decomposition framework, proves entropy contraction for both synchronous and asynchronous updates, establishes exponential ergodicity via a bootstrap argument, and concludes with a proof of algorithmic undecidability in higher dimensions.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to many-body dynamics and open quantum systems, as it provides rigorous bounds on how local noise and information exchange drive a system toward equilibrium (forgetting initial conditions) and explores the complexity of characterizing steady states.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
